page: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It includes 1.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.5 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2701.19 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the top 25%. However, due to its burstable allocation and limited memory, multi-core performance is weak, also scoring 2701.19 in stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is measured at 140.76 GB/sec, which is relatively poor. Other metrics reflect its entry-level nature, with a PassMark CPU score of 3130.58 and a static web serving throughput of 2.77 GB/sec.

This server type represents a highly cost-effective option for workloads with low resource demands or bursty CPU utilization patterns. The low memory density and burstable CPU allocation make it unsuitable for memory-intensive databases or sustained high-performance computing. It is best suited for lightweight applications, small-scale web serving, development environments, and basic background tasks that require occasional CPU bursts but otherwise remain idle.
description: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 1.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks but exhibits weak multi-core and memory performance due to its burstable CPU allocation and low memory density. It is a highly cost-effective option designed for lightweight workloads, development environments, and small-scale web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2ats_v2 is a burstable Basv2 virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, designed for cost-effective, low-intensity workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly burstable virtual machine with strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Configured with 2 burstable vCPUs and 1.0 GB of system memory
Delivers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core capacity
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and bursty tasks
